summary: Verified all 29 manifest hashes, line-level reviewed the three production sources and three test files, and independently re-ran the T-001 surface: tsc --noEmit exit 0; 19/19 T-001 tests green with real timing evidence (timeout kill ~2006ms, concurrency 2 batches ~4010ms, output-cap ~387ms). Grepped the entire src/ tree: the only child_process usage is execFile in probe-engine.ts (every 'spawn' hit is a comment or the "spawn-error" contract literal), so the REQ-003 no-exec boundary (security-spec B2 chokepoint) holds at source level. Allowlist is a frozen as-const table of exactly 14 bare-binary-name CLIs; no user input reaches command/args; resources bounded by maxBuffer=8KiB, timeout=2000ms+SIGKILL, concurrency=4. Red/Green TDD evidence is genuine (compile-failure-first exit 2 -> same 19-test surface exit 0). All eight scripted-gate logs green and consistent with sources. Scope matches the approved task exactly; only non-blocking Minor observations remain.

findings.description: Comment claims a 'backstop force-kill shortly after the timeout window' but no separate backstop timer is registered - only a child.on('error') handler. The kill is guaranteed by execFile's built-in timeout + killSignal:SIGKILL (independently confirmed ~2006ms). Doc/code mismatch, not a behavior defect.

A probe that exits 0 with empty/whitespace-only output is mapped to probeError='nonzero-exit' - a slight semantic imprecision; the value is a valid contract enum member and correctly yields available:false.
The verification contract's spec_revision field is empty (''); the evidence bundle carries the computed spec_revision, and the deterministic gates accept the contract. Metadata-only observation.
